subject: Driveway Replacement Permit — 422 Granville Ave
body_clean: Good afternoon Mayor Okonkwo, I'm planning to have my concrete driveway torn out and replaced at 422 Granville Ave before winter sets in. My contractor says I need a Village permit before he can start. Can someone point me to the application, the fee schedule, and whether there's an inspection required partway through the pour? I want to get this moving quickly — he has an opening the third week of October.

subject: Driveway Replacement Permit — 422 Granville Ave
body_clean: Ms. Szymanski, thank you for reaching out. A driveway replacement requires a Right-of-Way/Driveway Apron Permit from Community Development at Village Hall. The fee is $75 for up to 24 linear feet. Your contractor needs to submit a site sketch showing apron dimensions and any curb-cut work. An inspector does need to observe the base grade before the pour — call us at 708-555-0141 to schedule, usually same-week. You're well within time for an October project.

subject: Wood Privacy Fence — 1102 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Hi, I want to put up a 6-foot privacy fence along the south side and back of my yard at 1102 Bohland Ave. My neighbor and I already talked and we're good. I just need to know if a permit is required for a wooden fence that size and what the setback rules are from the property line. Trying to get this done before the ground freezes.

subject: Wood Privacy Fence — 1102 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Mr. Webb, fences over 5 feet require a Fence Permit under Village Code Section 12-4-8. Fee is $50. Your fence must be set back a minimum of 1 foot from the property line and the finished side must face outward. Submit your application with a survey or scaled plot plan showing fence placement. Rear and side yards may go to 6 feet; front yard is capped at 4 feet. Inspections are typically drive-by after installation. Applications accepted at Community Development, 3200 Washington Blvd.

subject: Water Heater Replacement — Do I Need a Permit?
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I had a plumber out yesterday and my water heater at 809 Harvard Ave is on its last legs. He can replace it next Tuesday but asked me to check whether Bellwood requires a permit for a residential water heater swap — same location, same size, just a newer unit. He's done work in other towns where they just install and then get an inspection, but he wasn't sure about Bellwood's process.

subject: Water Heater Replacement — Do I Need a Permit?
body_clean: Ms. Calabrese, yes — Bellwood requires a plumbing permit for water heater replacements. The fee is $45 and your licensed plumber must pull it before work begins; homeowners cannot self-permit on this one. He can apply online or in person at Village Hall. An inspection is required after installation and before the unit is put into service. Turnaround on issuance is usually one business day, so Tuesday is very doable if he applies today or Monday morning.

subject: Water Heater Replacement — Do I Need a Permit?
body_clean: Thank you so much, Tina. I passed that along to my plumber and he says he'll pull the permit Monday morning so we're set for Tuesday. Really appreciate how quickly you got back to me.

subject: Detached Garage Build — 1617 Morris Ave
body_clean: Hello, I'm hoping to build a one-car detached garage in my backyard at 1617 Morris Ave before the holidays. My brother-in-law is a licensed contractor so labor is covered. I know I need a building permit — I'm just trying to figure out what drawings the Village needs, how long review usually takes this time of year, and whether there are size limits I should know about. The footprint I'm planning is about 20x22 feet.

subject: Detached Garage Build — 1617 Morris Ave
body_clean: Mr. Montoya, a 20x22 garage is within the allowable accessory structure size for your zone. You'll need a Building Permit with a site plan showing setbacks (3 feet minimum from rear and side property lines), foundation plan, floor plan, and elevations. Your contractor must provide a Cook County license copy. Review runs 10-15 business days right now — I'd recommend submitting no later than November 15 to break ground this year. Fee is roughly $180-220 depending on final square footage.

subject: Permit Status Check — 350 Eastern Ave Fence
body_clean: I submitted a fence permit application for 350 Eastern Ave about three weeks ago and haven't heard anything back. The contractor I hired is asking me to reschedule if we don't have approval soon, and I really don't want to lose my spot on his calendar. Can someone look up where my application stands? My reference number was FP-2025-0847 if that helps.

subject: Permit Status Check — 350 Eastern Ave Fence
body_clean: Ms. Petersen, I pulled FP-2025-0847. It was approved on October 9th — the letter went to the email on file and appears to have landed in spam. The permit is valid through April 30, 2026, so your contractor has plenty of time. I'm re-sending the approval to the address you used on this message right now. Tell him he's cleared to proceed.

subject: Electrical Panel Upgrade at 2208 Madison St
body_clean: Good morning. I own 2208 Madison St and my electrician is recommending I upgrade the panel from 100A to 200A service before winter to handle a new heating system. He says he'll pull an electrical permit, but he wants me to confirm with the Village whether a separate mechanical or building permit is also needed for the service upgrade, or if the electrical permit covers everything.

subject: Electrical Panel Upgrade at 2208 Madison St
body_clean: Mr. Abrams, the electrical permit covers the panel and service entrance work. If the new heating system is a furnace or boiler, that requires a separate mechanical permit — fee starts at $65. Have your electrician and HVAC contractor each pull their own permits. Both can be submitted at the same time at Community Development. Turnaround is typically 2-3 business days for each.

subject: Inspector Never Came for My Driveway Pour — 714 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm frustrated and don't know who else to contact. I pulled a driveway permit in September for 714 Hirsch Ave. The instructions said to call for an inspection before the pour. I called twice, left messages, and nobody ever called back. My contractor waited two days and then had to pour or lose his crew. Now I'm worried I'll get a violation notice or have problems when I go to sell. What should I do?

subject: Inspector Never Came for My Driveway Pour — 714 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Ms. Olszewski, thank you for writing me directly. You did everything right by calling ahead, and an unreturned callback is not acceptable on our end. I'm asking our Community Development director to look into what happened and to schedule a post-pour inspection as soon as possible. In most cases where work was done in good faith, a post-pour inspection can close out the permit without issue. Someone will be in touch with you by end of this week.

subject: Garage Door Replacement — Permit Required?
body_clean: Hi — quick question. I'm replacing my garage door at 3015 Washington Blvd with a new insulated steel door, same opening size as the old one. My neighbor thinks a permit is required but the door company says it's just a replacement and no permit is needed. Can you clarify? I don't want a fine down the road.

subject: Garage Door Replacement — Permit Required?
body_clean: Mr. Rahimi, your door company is correct. A like-for-like garage door replacement — same opening size, no structural header changes — does not require a permit in Bellwood. If you were widening the opening, that would trigger a building permit. You're good to proceed. Keep the receipt and installation paperwork just in case.

subject: Chainlink Fence Repair at 88 Geneva Ave
body_clean: Hello. I am 73 and have lived at 88 Geneva Ave for over forty years. The back section of my chainlink fence blew down in a storm last month and I had a neighbor's grandson patch it temporarily. I'd like to hire a proper contractor to replace that whole back section — maybe 60 feet of it. My daughter says I should check if a permit is needed for fence repairs or only for new installations. Thank you for your time.

subject: Chainlink Fence Repair at 88 Geneva Ave
body_clean: Ms. Fitzpatrick, thank you for checking with us first. Replacing a section of existing fence in-kind — same material, same height, same location — qualifies as repair and does not require a permit. If the replacement changed the height or switched to a different material, a permit would apply. For a straight chainlink repair along your back property line your contractor can proceed without a permit. Best of luck with the project.

subject: Contractor Gone — Open Permit on Shed at 1930 50th Ave
body_clean: I had a permitted shed built in my backyard at 1930 50th Ave this past August. The permit is still open because my contractor never scheduled the final inspection — he just stopped responding about three weeks ago. I have the permit card still posted in the shed window. How do I get this closed out? I don't want an open permit hanging over the property when I go to refinance.

subject: Contractor Gone — Open Permit on Shed at 1930 50th Ave
body_clean: Mr. Tran, good news — the homeowner can call for the final inspection directly; you don't need the contractor. Call 708-555-0141 with your permit number and address and we'll schedule an inspector. If the shed matches the approved plans, it should close out with no issues. If there are minor items, we'll note them and give you a reasonable window to correct. Don't wait too long — permits do expire, though we can usually grant an extension for situations like yours.

subject: Emergency Furnace Replacement — Permit Not Yet Pulled
body_clean: Hi, my furnace at 1408 Bellwood Ave died last weekend and my HVAC company replaced it on an emergency basis Friday night. They said they'd pull the permit Monday. It's now Wednesday and they still haven't. Can I pull the mechanical permit myself as the homeowner, or does it have to go through them? And will there be a problem because the work is already done?

subject: Fence Permit Fee Seems Wrong — 615 St. Paul Ave
body_clean: I went in to pull a fence permit last week for 615 St. Paul Ave and was told the fee would be $110 because my fence is over 150 linear feet. The fee schedule posted online shows $50 flat for residential fences. I don't mind paying the right amount — I just want to understand how the calculation works before I hand over my check.

subject: Fence Permit Fee Seems Wrong — 615 St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Mr. Kuczewski, good catch. The $50 base fee covers runs up to 100 linear feet. For 101-200 feet, it's $50 plus $0.60 per linear foot over 100. At 150 feet that's $50 plus $30 — so $80 total, not $110. It sounds like there was a calculation error at the counter. I'm addressing this with staff. Please come back in and we'll process it correctly at $80.
